title: crepis setosa subsp. typica
intro: Crepis setosa subsp. typica, also known as the hairy hawk's beard or rough hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family, native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The health benefits of Crepis setosa subsp. typica include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Crepis setosa subsp. typica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and capsules, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential side effects and interactions.
